frozen_components: base CodeT5/CodeT5+ architecture (fine-tuned but not frozen)
architecture.summary: SliceT5 reformulates static program slicing as a sequence-to-sequence task using lightweight Transformer-based language models (CodeT5, CodeT5+). The system augments the base model with a copy mechanism that computes copy probability at each decoding step to directly transfer tokens from input to output, improving handling of rare identifiers and dependency capture. During inference, constrained beam search enforces two constraints: lexical constraint restricts output tokens to those present in the input, and syntactic constraint uses Tree Similarity of Edit Distance (TSED) monotonicity to detect and discard structurally invalid outputs. The copy mechanism operates via cross-attention weights to compute context vectors, which are concatenated with decoder input and passed through a learnable linear copy module to produce interpolated generation and copy distributions.
trained_components: special marker tokens for task-specific fine-tuning

biblio.title: SLICET5: Static Program Slicing using Language Models with Copy Mechanism and Constrained Decoding

claims.statement: SliceT5 with CodeT5+ improves ExactMatch by 6.4% on CodeNet and 27% on LeetCode compared to the best-performing baseline (NS-slicer)
SliceT5 with CodeT5+ achieves 98.32% Accuracy-D, 91.30% ExactMatch, 92.31% CodeBLEU, and 97.06% TSED on CodeNet
On the out-of-domain LeetCode dataset, SliceT5 with CodeT5+ achieves 70.85% Accuracy-D, 14% ExactMatch, 60.40% CodeBLEU, and 59.97% TSED
Without the copy mechanism, SliceT5 (CodeT5+) ExactMatch drops from 14% to 4% on out-of-domain LeetCode dataset
Without the lexical constraint, SliceT5 (CodeT5+) ExactMatch drops from 14% to 7% on out-of-domain LeetCode dataset

All components (copy mechanism, lexical constraint, and syntactic constraint) make significant contributions to SliceT5's performance, with copy mechanism and lexical constraint being the most impactful across datasets and metrics

SliceT5 demonstrates strong performance on incomplete code snippets (missing class encapsulation, missing semicolons, missing or unmatched braces), consistently outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and showing robustness in real-world development environments

Foundation models (GPT-4o-mini, Gemma-7B) achieve near-zero ExactMatch scores (0% on CodeNet with all prompting strategies), suffering from hallucinations and misinterpretation of control flow structures despite techniques like Chain-of-Thought and Retrieval-Augmented Generation
